_comment: Fixtures kymo intentionally diverges on, excluded from the headline accuracy score. legacy = deprecated/ambiguous Mermaid syntax that mermaid.js parses via its full grammar but kymo's lenient parser throws on. exotic = pathological parser stress fixtures / out-of-scope features (full KaTeX layout, decimal autonumber) that no real diagram uses. Each: dataset/grammar/file -> reason.

legacy.reason: inline edge label literally contains the link delimiter (A-- test text with == -->B); ambiguous without mermaid's full tokenizer
dotted inline edge label literally contains == (A-. test text with == .->B); same ambiguity
legacy asymmetric >...] shape chained with arrows (A_node-->odd->Vertex Text]); deprecated/ambiguous form
multiline edge label with embedded --" across physical lines (v10["<11"] --"...); ambiguous edge/label boundary

exotic.reason: double-backslash-escaped, deeply-nested KaTeX with custom commands (\phase, \relax, nested \Bigg/\begin{cases}/continued \frac); kymo has a lightweight TeX->Unicode pass, not a full KaTeX layout engine
decimal autonumber (autonumber 10.1 .01); kymo's autonumber counter is integer
mermaid renders a literal \n in a note (token "nDo"); kymo correctly folds it to a space
stress fixture with stray tokens before an aliased entity (buzz/fizz)
invalid  block inside an erDiagram (not ER syntax)
mermaid wraps very long class/member names at an exact pixel width into split word-tokens; kymo keeps them on one line (cannot replicate mermaid's text metrics)
mermaid wraps very long class/member names at an exact pixel width into split word-tokens; kymo keeps them on one line (cannot replicate mermaid's text metrics)
mermaid wraps very long mindmap labels at an exact pixel width into split word-tokens; kymo keeps them on one line
